item.title: Gorefast
item.author: estudioteh@gmail.com (Helena)
item.description:
Gorefast

Author: Helena(estudioteh@gmail.com)

GORE THEM ALL.

Probably my favorite thing to come out of the miserable little event that was the pandemic.

I’ve always loved Doom, Blood, and Duke. That early 90s era of first person shooters now jokingly called “Boomer shooters”, which I never quite understood. I feel I walk the fine line between being a millenial and a zoomer, but these sorts of games were still my childhood!

Gorefast is, if anything, a love letter to all of these FPS games. It originally started as just a way to pass time, evolved into what was going to be essentially left 4 dead but 90s aesthetic, feature-crept into something unwieldly, and got cut down into a neat little 4 level FPS game.

Published on 2026-06-03 at algumaaaa..., last modified on 2026-06-03

item.title: Coagula Subita
item.author: estudioteh@gmail.com (Helena)
item.description:
Coagula Subita

Author: Helena(estudioteh@gmail.com)

How would one compose silence?

As someone who’s often composing quite noisy material, both in the loudness and textural sense, I’ve always found myself intrigued by quiet pieces. Cage’s 4'33" is an obvious example that comes to mind but I’m also refering to minimal ambient compositions, or whispered vocals, or synthesizers so shy they almost seem lonely.

This piece didn’t necessarily start with this objective in mind. As all things in life tend to be it was a chaotic mix of random ideas that would prop up on a day and disappear just as easy the next. Samples taken from things that were breaking in my apartment, and in my university, and on museums. It wasn’t until I started to lay out these sounds in my DAW that I heard it: The silence after listening to these often loud samples was mesmerizing! And it clicked.

Published on 2026-06-01 at algumaaaa..., last modified on 2026-06-01

item.title: Monster Hunter World Iceborne Review
item.author: estudioteh@gmail.com (Helena)
item.description:
Monster Hunter World Iceborne Review

Author: Helena(estudioteh@gmail.com)

One massive, breathtaking leap forward that didn’t quite stick the landing.

This might be one of the most difficult games to rate of anything i’ve ever played. At times its a solid 4 with incredibly well done redesign of old monsters together with interesting new aditions to the cast. The quality of life features helping make the experience all the more smoother and the moment to moment gameplay feeling more polished than ever before. And then you’re out of the hunt, and thrust into the tangled mess of a web that are the game’s sorry excuse for monster hunter systems.

Published on 2026-05-31 at algumaaaa..., last modified on 2026-05-31
